---
title: "How I Structured My Project"
subtitle: "A big-picture overview of the SSC code"
image: "../../man/figures/structure.png"
date: last-modified
format: html
---

This post gives a high-level overview of how the entire SSC project is organised.  
The project uses a simple but robust structure:

- **a central `main.R` file**, from which the full pipeline is executed;  
- **modular comparison scripts**, one per design;  
- **a dedicated `functions/` directory** for all helpers and wrappers;  
- **a small S7 layer** holding the two core classes (`ssc_design` and `ssc_results`).

## Directory Structure

```default
R/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ init.R
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ main.R
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ comparison/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ comp_bin_fixed_exact.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ comp_bin_fixed_pooled.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ comp_bin_gs.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ comp_bin_one_arm_exact.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ comp_bin_one_arm.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ comp_surv_fixed.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ comp_surv_gs.R
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ comp_surv_one_arm.R
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ functions/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ checks.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ e_ratio.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ evaluate_relevancy.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ helpers.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ n_ratio.R
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ exact_wrapper.R
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ S7/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ generics.R
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ssc_design.R
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ ssc_results.R
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ side-analysis/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ bin2arms_exact.R
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ lakatos_n_lan.R
в””в”Ђв”Ђ wrappers/
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ahern_wrapper.R
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ bbssr_wrapper.R
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ exact_wrapper.R
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ gsdesign2_wrapper.R
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ oa2s_wrapper.R
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ rashnu_wrapper.R
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ rpact_wrapper.R
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ sssas_wrapper.R
```

## main.R: the entry point

`main.R` is the **orchestrator** of the entire project.  
Running this file executes all steps in the predefined order:

-   initialization,
-   survival comparisons,
-   binary comparisons,
-   result combination,
-   exporting final outputs.

This is described in detail in the post:  
**вћЎпёЏ [*вЂњMy main pipelineвЂќ*](pipeline.qmd)**

## init.R: preparing the environment

`init.R` configures everything needed for the project:

-   loads all required packages,
-   creates global objects (e.g. `ssc`, `wrapper`, error rate),
-   sources helper functions,
-   loads all wrapper functions,
-   loads S7 classes and methods.

## Comparison scripts (comp_*.R)

Each script corresponds to **one design Г— one endpoint** and follows the same structure:

1.  build parameter grid
2.  create a `ssc_design` object
3.  compute results using wrappers
4.  validate with S7
5.  combine results
6.  compute derived metrics (NвЂ‘ratio, relevancy)
7.  generate tables & plots
8.  export everything inside the `ssc` object

This standard pattern is what makes the pipeline extensible: adding a new design simply means adding a new `comp_xyz.R` following the same recipe.

## The functions/ directory

This directory contains all **reusable building blocks** of the project:

### **Core helpers**

-   `checks.R` вЂ” input validation
-   `helpers.R` вЂ” general utilities
-   `evaluate_relevancy.R` вЂ” rule defining вЂњhigh / medium / lowвЂќ relevancy
-   `n_ratio.R` вЂ” NвЂ‘Ratio computation

### **Wrappers for external R packages**

-   `rpact_wrapper.R`
-   `bbssr_wrapper.R`
-   `gsdesign2_wrapper.R`
-   `rashnu_wrapper.R`
-   `ahern_wrapper.R`
-   `exact_2_arms.R` вЂ” exact binomial implementation
-   `sssas_wrapper.R`
-   `oa2s_wrapper.R`

This part is discussed in detail in the post:  
**вћЎпёЏ [*вЂњClean Wrapper Functions for Sample Size CalculationsвЂќ*](wrapper.qmd)**

## The S7 layer (`S7/`)

This folder contains a minimal but powerful object system for the project:

*   **`ssc_design.R`** вЂ” defines how a design is represented
*   **`ssc_results.R`** вЂ” defines how results are stored & validated
*   **`generics.R`** вЂ” shared S7 methods (print, summary, extractors, etc.)

This is discussed in detail in the post:  
**вћЎпёЏ [*вЂњS7 Classes for Designs and ResultsвЂќ*](S7.qmd)**
